title: Fabila-Monroy et al. Conjecture (2009)
context_text: Fabila-Monroy et al. [10] first studied the chromatic number of the associahedron $\mathcal{A}_n$, gave an explicit $\lceil{\frac{n}{2}}\rceil$-colouring, and observed $\chi(\mathcal{A}_n)\in O(n/\log n)$ via Johansson's result on triangle-free graphs. They conjectured the stronger $O(\log n)$ upper bound. No non-constant lower bound is known; the best known lower bound is $\chi(\mathcal{A}_{10})\geqslant 4$.
attributed_to: Fabila-Monroy, Flores-Peñaloza, Huemer, Hurtado, Urrutia, and Wood
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full by establishing $\chi(\mathcal{A}_n)\in O(\log n)$ as Theorem 1.
paper_title: A logarithmic bound for the chromatic number of the associahedron
